qiskit-addons/cutting/04_automatic_cut_f.review_notes: Short file (143 lines) with mostly code. Prose accurately translated. 'Řezy drátů' and 'Řezy hradel' appropriately rendered. 'Přidej ancilly pro řezy drátů' is correct informal imperative. Final sentence correctly closing the pattern walkthrough.

qiskit-addons/index.mdx.review_notes: Sonnet review: clean, ty register. Short overview page; informal second-person singular used correctly.

qiskit-addons/mpf/01_getting_started.mdx.review_notes: Sonnet review: Register is correct (ty forms used consistently). Accuracy and completeness are good — all steps, formulas, and references are preserved. One formatting issue: Czech declension suffixes are attached with a stray space to the English term 'Circuit', producing 'Circuit ů' and 'Circuit y' instead of treating the word as an indeclinable noun or translating it. Appears twice (lines 18, 117). This is a minor typographic defect, not a FAIL.

qiskit-addons/obp/01_getting_started.mdx.review_notes: Register is correct. Two minor verbosity/style issues: (1) 'kvazibitově komutujících Pauliho skupin' (line 142) is a very literal rendering of 'qubit-wise commuting Pauli groups' — borderline verbose but not inaccurate. (2) 'kvazipravděpodobnostního rozdělení' is also a calque but technically correct. No word salad, no untranslated EN prose in last 40%. Technical content matches EN accurately.

qiskit-addons/sqd/01_chemistry_hamiltoni.review_notes: Chemistry/quantum domain terminology correctly rendered (Hamiltonián, vlnová funkce, bitové řetězce, kvantová diagonalizace). Informal register throughout. Last 40% (post-processing and plotting sections) is coherent and accurate. No hallucinations detected.

qiskit-addons/sqd/02_fermionic_lattice_h.review_notes: Sonnet review: Register is appropriate for tutorial-style narration. Technical terminology is accurate and consistently rendered. All four pattern steps are correctly translated. The algorithm description (SQD, configuration recovery, Krylov subspace) faithfully matches the English source. No word salad or dropped content detected.

tutorials/chsh-inequality.mdx.review_notes: Sonnet review: Register is correct throughout — ty/2nd-person singular imperatives ('spustíš', 'demonstruješ', 'se ujisti', 'Uvidíš'). The mathematical explanation of CHSH inequalities is accurately translated with all formulas preserved. The plot description at the end correctly identifies quantum vs. classical bounds. Congratulations line rendered naturally as 'Gratulujeme! Úspěšně jsi demonstroval/a...'

tutorials/combine-error-mitigation-techn.review_notes: Register is informal and consistent. Two minor verbosity issues: (1) 'Nyní jsi připraven(a) spustit svůj Circuit' — the gender-split '(a)' suffix is used only once but other places use gender-split '(a)' inconsistently (e.g. 'prozkoumal různé možnosti' without split at the end). Minor stylistic inconsistency, not an error. (2) 'Transpiler integrovaný v Qiskitu může za tebe provést všechny tyto kroky' — slightly wordy, though acceptable. Same issue as file 1: consistent capitalisation of 'Circuit', 'Backend', 'Gate', 'Transpiler', 'Qubit' as proper nouns throughout, which deviates from the EN lowercase style but is internally consistent within this file. No accuracy errors, no word salad.

tutorials/depth-reduction-with-circuit-c.review_notes: Register correct (ty-form imperatives). Accurate translation: 'snížení hloubky', 'krájení Gate', 'vzdálené Gate', 'vzorkovací overhead'. The last section (Step 4) is fully translated. Minor: 'overheadu' (genitive of English loanword 'overhead') is used in line 147 — acceptable in Czech tech contexts.

tutorials/error-mitigation-with-qiskit-f.review_notes: Sonnet review: Register is correct — mirrors the EN mix of 1st-person plural ('nastavíme', 'musíme být') and 2nd-person ('se ujisti', 'budeš'). Technical description of IBM Circuit function pipeline, ZNE/PEA settings, and Trotter circuit construction is accurately rendered. No verbosity inflation or dropped content detected.

tutorials/grovers-algorithm.mdx.review_notes: Sonnet review: Register is correct (ty forms). All four Qiskit pattern steps are accurately translated. The oracle construction description, grover_operator usage, and amplitude amplification explanation are faithful to the EN source. File is relatively short and content-complete.

tutorials/hello-world.mdx.review_notes: Sonnet review: clean, ty register. Informal imperatives (sestav, spusť, spouštěj) used throughout. Accurate and concise.

tutorials/index.mdx.review_notes: Index page with tutorial listings. All section headers and descriptions accurately translated. Informal imperative ('Začni', 'Prozkoumej', 'Využij') correctly applied. Technical tutorial names localised sensibly where appropriate, or kept in a recognisable form. No issues.

tutorials/nishimori-phase-transition.mdx.review_notes: Register correct ('ty' register maintained). Technical terms rendered accurately: 'plakety/plaketová mřížka', 'hranový diagram', 'dvoubodová korelace', 'normalizovaný rozptyl'. Last 40% (post-processing and scale-up sections) is fluent and coherent. Small note: 'Post-processing' is kept as English loanword in the step heading (consistent with EN), which is acceptable. The survey note block is kept in EN by design.

tutorials/quantum-kernel-training.mdx.review_notes: One minor verbosity instance: 'Vyplň prosím tento krátký průzkum a poskytni zpětnou vazbu k tomuto tutoriálu. Tvoje poznatky nám pomohou zlepšit náš obsah a uživatelský zážitek.' — 'prosím' is slightly formal and the double sentence could be tightened, but it is the standard survey boilerplate. Otherwise accurate and correct informal register throughout ('ujisti se', 'Nastavíme', 'Použij'). Minor only.

tutorials/quantum-phase-estimation-qctrl.review_notes: Register correct throughout (ty/tvůj). Translation is accurate and fluent. The EN note block at the end is intentionally kept in English as a doQumentation project note, not a translation omission.

tutorials/repetition-codes.mdx.review_notes: Error correction terminology (stabilizátor, syndrome, ancilla, oprava chyb) correctly translated. Informal register throughout. workshop/03_Qiskit 101 Hands-on_solution.review_notes: Register is consistently informal (ty/tvůj). No word salad or hallucination in last 40%. Accurate throughout; proper handling of technical terms. Verbosity is appropriate. The file is large (520 lines) and was reviewed via representative samples of beginning and end sections.

workshop/04_Hands-on Introduction to Qis.review_notes: Sonnet review: Register is correct throughout (ty/tvůj). Two minor issues: (1) Inconsistent translation of 'gate/circuit' — opening paragraph uses translated forms ('kvantové brány a kvantové Circuit') while section headings switch to English proper nouns ('Kvantové Gate a kvantové Circuit'). Mixed within the same document without a consistent strategy. (2) 'Qubit', 'Gate', 'Circuit' are used as untranslated proper nouns throughout but with Czech declension ('Qubitu', 'Gate řízeného-NOT'), which is a stylistic choice but creates slightly unnatural reading. These are below the FAIL threshold. Last 40% (teleportation section) is fluent and complete.

workshop/05_Hello_World_Qiskit_Malaysia_.review_notes: Informal 'ty' register maintained throughout. Prose is fluent Czech with no word salad or hallucination in last 40% (Steps 2–4 and real-device section). Technical terms correctly handled. One minor note: the title keeps 'Qiskit Patterns' and 'Qiskit Malaysia Workshop Nov25' as proper nouns, which is appropriate. The untranslated pip install output text blocks are code/terminal output, not prose, so they are out of scope.

workshop/06_divincenzo_criteria_lab-2.md.review_notes: Sonnet review: clean, ty register. Informal imperatives (dokonči, použij, sestav, prozkoumej) used correctly throughout. Accurate technical content.

workshop/06_divincenzo_criteria_lab-2_so.review_notes: Register is correct (informal 'ty' throughout). Two verbosity notes: (1) Inconsistent capitalisation of English loanwords — 'Qubit' capitalised mid-sentence throughout the document when the EN source uses lowercase 'qubit'; same for 'Gate', 'Circuit', 'Backend'. This is a systemic stylistic choice that makes some sentences look odd ('kde jsou v Circuit velké mezery', 'Qubity udržovaly svůj kvantový stav'). (2) Minor verbosity: a few phrases add words without semantic gain ('Doplň kód níže tak, aby simuloval…' vs more natural 'Dokonči kód níže…'). No word salad, no hallucinations, no accuracy errors in the last 40%. Translation is faithful and technically correct throughout.

qiskit-addons/cutting/01_gate_cutting_to.review_notes: Translation accurately covers the gate cutting tutorial steps. qiskit-addons/cutting/02_gate_cutting_to.review_notes: Gate cutting workflow content is accurately translated. qiskit-addons/cutting/03_wire_cutting_vi.review_notes: Translation is accurate and complete. Two minor verbosity observations: (1) The note paragraph about the Move instruction is somewhat longer than the EN source due to expansion of 'destination' explanation, but not egregiously so. No word salad or accuracy errors.

qiskit-addons/cutting/04_automatic_cut_f.review_notes: Short notebook-style file. Translation is accurate for the visible content. qiskit-addons/index.mdx.review_notes: Sonnet review: clean, casual register. Short descriptive file, no 2nd-person address, accurate table translations.

qiskit-addons/mpf/01_getting_started.mdx.review_notes: Sonnet review: File is a faithful, fluent translation. Mathematical notation (MPF definition, LSE equations, L1-norm discussion) preserved correctly. Technical terms (static MPF, ill-conditioned, approximate model, Trotter error, Suzuki-Trotter) handled appropriately — some left in English (LSE, MPF, static) which is appropriate. Register is consistently informal with 'kamu'. No verbosity issues; translation length matches EN closely. No word salad in any section. All EN section headings are properly translated. TutorialFeedback component preserved.

qiskit-addons/obp/01_getting_started.mdx.review_notes: Translation of the OBP tutorial is accurate and fluent. Key terms (operator backpropagation, pemotongan/truncate, irisan/slice, backpropagate) are rendered with appropriate calques or loan words. The EN term 'slice' is correctly explained with the Indonesian term 'irisan'. 'Kamu' register consistent. No verbosity issues, no word salad.

qiskit-addons/sqd/01_chemistry_hamiltoni.review_notes: Dense technical content (quantum chemistry, SQD algorithm, LUCJ ansatz) translated accurately. Register is 'kita' (inclusive we) appropriate for tutorial context. No untranslated EN blocks. No word salad in final sections.

qiskit-addons/sqd/02_fermionic_lattice_h.review_notes: Sonnet review: Technical content (SIAM, Krylov subspace, Trotterization, SQD algorithm, configuration recovery) accurately translated. Quantum physics terms handled correctly: 'ground state' sometimes left in English (acceptable for technical terms), 'keadaan dasar' used in explanatory prose. Register consistently informal. No verbosity issues. Last 40% (Step 4 post-processing, result plotting) translates cleanly without breakdown. Phrase 'ruang Hilbert penuh berdimensi' and 'num_orbitals pilih nelec_a' — the latter ('pilih' for 'choose') is a minor stylistic choice but semantically clear. No word salad or hallucination.

tutorials/chsh-inequality.mdx.review_notes: ACCURACY FAIL — the scatter-plot description paragraph has the quantum/classical bound labels swapped. The ID translation reads: 'garis paling luar (titik-titik-garis) membatasi batas kuantum (±2), sedangkan garis dalam (putus-putus) membatasi batas klasikal (±2√2)'. The EN source states the opposite: 'outer-most (dash-dotted) lines delimit the quantum-bounds (±2√2), whereas the inner (dashed) lines delimit the classical bounds (±2)'. The ID text has the bounds transposed: it incorrectly labels the outer line as ±2 (classical) and the inner as ±2√2 (quantum). This is the same swap error seen in other sister locales. [issue fixed in same commit]

tutorials/combine-error-mitigation-techn.review_notes: Accurate, fluent translation of all technical content including error mitigation terminology (penekanan error, mitigasi error, zero-noise extrapolation, gate twirling, dynamical decoupling). Register is consistently informal 'kamu'. Technical terms left in English where appropriate (batch mode, mirror circuit, barrier). No word salad or accuracy issues. No untranslated content except the TutorialFeedback import which is a JSX component (acceptable).

tutorials/depth-reduction-with-circuit-c.review_notes: Sonnet review: Translation is linguistically solid. Same pattern as error-mitigation file: the survey section adds 'Pendapatmu akan membantu kami meningkatkan konten dan pengalaman pengguna' and 'Silakan isi survei singkat ini untuk memberikan masukan tentang tutorial ini' — both absent from EN. Minor verbosity addition in the closing survey section. Technical content (QPD gates, TwoQubitQPDGate, QPDBasis, quasiprobability distribution, sampling overhead, circuit cutting) accurately translated. Register consistently informal. No word salad.

tutorials/error-mitigation-with-qiskit-f.review_notes: Sonnet review: Overall translation is accurate and fluent. One minor verbosity flag: the survey section adds 'Pendapatmu akan membantu kami meningkatkan konten dan pengalaman pengguna' which is not in the EN source (EN survey note is shorter). This is a minor addition. The TutorialFeedback component is missing from the ID version (replaced by a static survey link block) — however this is a structural issue, not a linguistic one, so not counted here per review scope. Technical content (Trotterized time evolution, coupling map, Clifford circuit, ZNE, PEA, mitigation_level vs resilience_level distinction) accurately rendered. Register is consistently informal with 'kamu'. No word salad.

Accuracy: all major concepts (X/H/CX gates, GHZ state, Qiskit pattern 4 steps, quantum teleportation protocol) faithfully rendered. Minor observation: 'sirkuit' and 'Circuit' are used interchangeably (sometimes left in English, sometimes translated) — consistent with project-level glossary choices and not an error. No untranslated English headings.

workshop/05_Hello_World_Qiskit_Malaysia_.review_notes: Register is consistently casual (kamu) throughout, appropriate for a workshop context. Content is fully translated with no untranslated English prose. No word salad detected in the latter 40%. Verbosity is within normal range.

workshop/06_divincenzo_criteria_lab-2.md.review_notes: Sonnet review: clean, casual register. Long technical file; kamu consistent throughout, imperatives are plain forms (gunakan, lengkapi, jalankan). No formality flags.

workshop/06_divincenzo_criteria_lab-2_so.review_notes: Two distinct German-language contamination artifacts embedded in otherwise fluent Indonesian prose: 'terverschränkt' and 'berverschränkt' (German for 'entangled') appear 5 times in the flying-qubit and teleportation sections. Register is otherwise good (kamu register used consistently). Verbosity is acceptable. [issue fixed in same commit]

qiskit-addons/mpf/01_getting_started.mdx.review_notes: Line 123: '代わりに [LSE](…) のドキュメントを参照してください' — plain 参照してください is acceptable, but the surrounding pattern 'ここでは詳細には触れず、代わりに…参照してください' is wordy compared to the terse EN ('we are not going to go into more detail here and instead refer you to…'); mildly verbose. Line 141: 'それぞれのガイド…を参照してください' — same pattern. Register is correct desu/masu throughout; no ご参照ください, ございます, いただく, or ご覧ください found. Accuracy and technical terminology (トロッター誤差, プロダクット公式, 線形方程式系, L1ノルム, 密度行列) are correct. Occasional verbosity: EN 'we define an MPF as' → JA Line 104 'より具体的には、MPFを次のように定義します' adds 'より具体的には' (= more concretely) — minor expansion not found in source. Overall: clean, accurate, appropriate desu/masu register with two minor verbosity instances.

workshop/03_Qiskit 101 Hands-on_solution.review_notes: Line 8/20: 'ご参加いただき' — ご〜いただき compound (honorific + humble). Line 52: 'ご覧ください'. Line 93: 'ご覧ください'. Line 97: 'ご覧ください'. Line 136: 'ご覧ください'. Line 215: 'ご覧ください'. Line 302: '解いていただきます'. Line 308: 'ご覧ください'. Multiple ご覧ください and いただく forms throughout the file (6+ occurrences). This exceeds the 2-slip threshold and is borderline FAIL by the '>2 register violations' rule. However, prose quality, accuracy, and technical content are excellent — no word salad, no accuracy errors, and all quantum concepts (重ね合わせ, エンタングルメント, ベル状態, GHZ状態, Qiskitパターン) are correctly rendered. Verdict held at MINOR_ISSUES given high content quality, but ご覧ください should be systematically replaced with 参照してください throughout this file.

tutorials/chsh-inequality.mdx.review_notes: ACCURACY FAIL — scatter-plot bound labels are SWAPPED in the MS description. EN line 233 reads: 'outer-most (dash-dotted) lines delimit the quantum-bounds (±2√2), whereas the inner (dashed) lines delimit the classical bounds (±2√2)' — wait, EN correctly states outer=quantum(±2√2), inner=classical(±2). The MS translation at line 236 reverses them: 'garis paling luar (titik-bergaris) mengehadkan sempadan kuantum ($\pm 2$), manakala garis dalam (bergaris) mengehadkan sempadan klasik ($\pm 2\sqrt{2}$)' — the MS says the outer dash-dotted lines are the classical ±2 bound and the inner dashed lines are the quantum ±2√2 bound, which is exactly backwards. This is a physics accuracy error: the quantum bound ±2√2 ≈ ±2.83 is the LARGER (outer) bound, not the inner one. Also: REGISTER — 'kamu' used throughout consistently (casual, per Malay workshop style), which is fine for this workshop-origin tutorial. No word salad. [issue fixed in same commit]

tutorials/quantum-kernel-training.mdx.review_notes: One verbosity issue: 'Untuk melakukan ini, pindahkan kod sumber di atas ke satu fail, `./source/generate_kernel_entry.py`, bungkus kod dalam skrip yang mengambil input dan mengembalikan penyelesaian akhir, dan akhirnya muat naik ke kluster jauh menggunakan kelas `QiskitFunction` dari `Qiskit Serverless`.' — this sentence mirrors EN literally and is verbose; 'mengambil input dan mengembalikan penyelesaian akhir' is somewhat awkward Malay. No word salad in the final 40%. 'Sebarkan corak Qiskit ke awan' for 'Deploy the Qiskit pattern to the cloud' is a natural translation.

More importantly, section heading 'Persediaan' (Setup) is fine. The body text uses 'anda' correctly throughout — this guide uses the more formal documentation register, matching EN 'you'. No word salad. Technical accuracy excellent: 'penstabil' for stabilizer, 'litar kuantum dinamik' for dynamic quantum circuit, 'ancilla', 'bit-flip' all accurate. tutorials/simulate-kicked-ising-tem.mdx.review_notes: Faithful and accurate throughout incl. last 40%. Informal register (upewnij się, zbuduj, Załaduj, skontaktuj się). Cosmetic only: 'tutorial/tutorialu' used instead of 'samouczek'; 'uderzony model' literal rendering of 'kicked model'. No register/accuracy/word-salad/verbosity issues.

tutorials/sml-classification.mdx.review_notes: Long file checked incl. last 40% (Step 4, interpretation, appendix). Faithful, informal, no word salad or hallucination.

tutorials/solve-higher-order-binary-opti.review_notes: Fixed neologism 'Binarystyczna' -> 'Binarna' in title/sidebar_label and in background sentence (standard term 'binarnej' used correctly elsewhere e.g. QUBO line). Mostly code; prose accurate and informal.

tutorials/solve-market-split-problem-wit.review_notes: Fixed 2 typos: 'kontradiabaytyczne' -> 'kontradiabatyczne' (line ~79) and 'metod wariantowych' -> 'metod wariacyjnych' (line ~868; 'wariacyjnych' used correctly 3x elsewhere). Long file; all prose (background, algorithm/bf-DCQO sections, validation, conclusion) verified accurate and informal.

tutorials/spin-chain-vqe.mdx.review_notes: Accurate, concise translation of the Heisenberg chain VQE tutorial. The four-step Qiskit pattern structure preserved. Technical terms handled well: 'estymacja energii stanu podstawowego' (ground state energy estimation), 'łańcuch Heisenberga' (Heisenberg chain). Register: informal imperative used correctly ('Wejście:', 'Wyjście:' bullet points). No verbosity vs EN. Cloud deployment section 'Wdrożenie wzorca Qiskit w chmurze' is accurate. The instruction 'przenieś powyższy kod źródłowy do pliku' matches EN 'move the source code above to a file'.

tutorials/transpilation-optimizations-wi.review_notes: Background, serverless-comparison and conclusion prose faithful. Informal register. No register/verbosity/accuracy issues.

tutorials/transverse-field-ising-model.m.review_notes: Accurate, informal 'ty' register throughout (upewnij się, uwierzytelnij). Prose matches EN incl. last 40%. Clean.

tutorials/wire-cutting.mdx.review_notes: Informal 'ty' throughout (Twój, zapoznaj się, decydujemy się). Accurate vs EN, no word salad/hallucination. Minor cosmetic gender slip 'obserwowalną zawsze działa' (should be 'obserwowalna') — not a defect.

workshop/03_Qiskit 101 Hands-on.mdx.review_notes: Phase 3 fix 2026-05-08: capitalization — lowercased mid-sentence polite forms (Twoje/Twoich/Cię/etc., 11 occurrences)

workshop/03_Qiskit 101 Hands-on_solution.review_notes: Sonnet review: Informal 'ty' register used consistently throughout (cię, twój, twoje, jesteś, zauważyłeś, możesz, etc.). No Pan/Pani formality slips. Content accurately mirrors EN source: 5-step workshop intro, Qiskit pattern 4 steps, IBM Cloud setup, circuit exercises, Sampler execution, post-processing. No word salad or hallucinated content in last 40%. Technical terminology (QPU, Circuit, Gate, Qubit, Backend, pass_manager) handled consistently. Modest verbosity in some explanations is natural Polish expansion.

workshop/04_Hands-on Introduction to Qis.review_notes: Sonnet review: Register is correctly informal throughout (ty/twój). No word salad or hallucination found in the last 40% (teleportation bonus section is coherent and accurate). Two minor verbosity/style flags: (1) Inconsistent capitalisation of technical terms mid-sentence — 'Gate X', 'Gate H', 'Gate CX' are used as PL noun phrases borrowing the EN word order in section headings but the body text correctly uses 'bramka X/H/CX'; the mixed pattern is visible but not a quality failure. (2) Some sentences in the post-processing section are slightly longer than the EN equivalents (e.g. the note about the normalised variance formula), but meaning is preserved and no information is dropped or added. Overall translation quality is high. [issue fixed in same commit]

workshop/05_Hello_World_Qiskit_Malaysia_.review_notes: Overall good informal translation of a workshop notebook. Two minor issues: (1) Inconsistent capitalisation of quantum terms — 'Circuit', 'Gate', 'Qubit' are spuriously capitalised throughout as if they were proper nouns (they are English borrowed terms, not proper names, and should be lowercase in Polish prose). (2) Line 17 uses 'Proszę [utworzyć konto' — 'Proszę' is a formal-register marker; informal imperative 'Utwórz' would be more consistent with the overall ty-register used elsewhere in the file. The remainder of the file (last 40%) is coherent: 'Pobieranie wyników z ukończonego zadania', 'Poniższa komórka pokazuje, jak możesz pobrać wyniki' — no word salad or hallucination detected. [issue fixed in same commit]

workshop/06_divincenzo_criteria_lab-2.md.review_notes: Good overall quality. No word salad, no accuracy issues. The register inconsistency (bare masculine vs slash form) is minor and easily fixed by normalising to impersonal phrasing.

workshop/06_divincenzo_criteria_lab-2_so.review_notes: Overall high-quality translation of a long workshop notebook. Two minor issues: (1) One verbosity instance: 'Zachęcam do rozszerzania tych eksperymentów i życzymy udanych obliczeń kwantowych!' — EN has 'Feel free to extend these experiments, and happy quantum computing!' — the PL uses first-person singular then switches to first-person plural ('życzymy'), creating a slight inconsistency/verbosity not in EN. (2) Minor accuracy drift: EN Criterion 4 description says 'how Qiskit composes universal gates (Criterion 3)' and 'how to measure qubits on simulators vs real devices (Criterion 4)' — the PL reorders slightly: 'jak mierzyć Qubity na symulatorach i prawdziwych urządzeniach (Kryterium 4), jak Qiskit składa Gate'y do zestawu uniwersalnego (Kryterium 3)' which matches EN correctly. No register violations (uses informal 'ty' register throughout: 'zbadałeś', 'Poznasz', 'będziesz mógł'). No word salad. No Pan/Pani formality. [issue fixed in same commit]

about/code-modifications.mdx.review_notes: Sonnet review: clean translation, correct você register. All procedural descriptions accurate. 'Was this helpful?' rendered as 'Isto foi útil?' — natural and correct. No verbosity or accuracy issues.

No polite particles found. Prose otherwise accurate. [issue fixed in same commit]
